risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to limited vent holes.
risk.mit: Increase number of vent holes for better thermal management.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient stiffness in deployer rails.
risk.mit: Reinforce deployer rails with additional stiffeners.

risk.desc: Possible misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Tighten manufacturing tolerances to IT6.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate electrical routing.
risk.mit: Optimize PCB layout for better signal integrity.

risk.desc: Potential structural failure under extreme loads.
risk.mit: Conduct load tests and reinforce critical areas.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: 3U frame, deployer rails, board stack support
physics_paths: Load path: Corner posts distribute loads from solar panels and antennas.
Flow path: Airflow through vent holes for thermal management.
Thermal path: Heat dissipation from electronics via aluminum fins.
Motion path: Rail guides allow deployment of external components.
Pressure path: Internal pressure equalization through vent holes.
Electrical path: Power and data routing through PCB traces and connectors.
Human/service access path: Access panels for maintenance and testing.
integration_opportunities: Enhance thermal management with additional cooling fins.
Improve structural integrity with additional stiffeners.
Optimize power distribution with integrated circuit boards.
Increase deployer rail robustness with reinforced guides.
verification_focus: Mass verification: Ensure total mass matches budget.
Dimensional verification: Confirm all parts fit within specified tolerances.
Functional verification: Test deployer rail functionality.
Thermal verification: Validate heat dissipation performance.
Electrical verification: Verify power and data routing integrity.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion mismatch between aluminum and carbon fiber components.
risk.mit: Use thermal analysis software to simulate and mitigate any adverse effects.

risk.desc: Burn wire release mechanism may not activate properly due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough testing on prototypes before final production.

risk.desc: Deployment damper could fail if not properly calibrated.
risk.mit: Include a calibration step during assembly and perform regular inspections.

risk.desc: Service access window might interfere with adjacent subsystems if not precisely positioned.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the position during the design phase to ensure compatibility.

risk.desc: Fastener insert pattern may not align perfectly with mating parts due to cumulative tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement a tolerance stack-up analysis to predict and correct potential misalignments.

physics_paths.path_description: Electrical energy generated by solar panels is routed through flexible harness to charge controller.
Thermal energy absorbed by solar panels is dissipated via heat sink fins.
Mechanical force from deployment mechanism moves solar panel wing via hinge joint.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to mixed materials (Al 7075 and CFRP).
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material compatibility.

risk.desc: Inspection window may compromise structural integrity if not properly reinforced.
risk.mit: Add reinforcement ribs around the inspection window cutout.

risk.desc: Lens mount may require precise alignment which could be challenging during assembly.
risk.mit: Include alignment pins and guide features for easier assembly.

risk.desc: Data connector may face accessibility challenges due to limited space.
risk.mit: Optimize connector placement and ensure adequate clearance for tools.

risk.desc: Strain relief for data cables might be difficult to implement within tight constraints.
risk.mit: Use preformed strain relief components designed for confined spaces.
